answer:
**Manusmṛti chapter 5 does not prescribe any specific kind of wood (or fuel of any kind) for the fire used in cooking funeral offerings (such as piṇḍas or rice balls for ancestors).**

Chapter 5 (verses roughly Mn 4.196–5.96 in standard numbering, based on the translation here) focuses primarily on dietary rules for twice-born castes: forbidden foods (e.g., garlic, onions, certain meats, village birds); permissible meats in sacrifices or for sustenance; rules for eating in ancestral/god rites; penances for violations; and gifts.[^1] It mentions rites for ancestors (*pitṛs*) and gods several times (e.g., meat allowed therein), but nothing about cooking funeral/ancestral offerings (*śrāddha*) or the wood/fuel for any fire used in them.[^2]

Searches across the full Manusmṛti (and related Smṛtis) for terms like "wood," "fuel," "sticks" (*samidh*), "fire," "cook"/"boil," "funeral offerings," *piṇḍa* ("rice ball"), or *śrāddha* yielded no matches in chapter 5 linking them. *Piṇḍas* appear elsewhere (e.g., ch. 4 on monthly *śrāddhas*), but not with cooking instructions here.[^3] Sacrificial sticks (*samidh*) are noted in other Smṛtis like Yājñavalkya Smṛti (e.g., palāśa, udumbara), but not prescribed in Manusmṛti ch. 5.[^4]

If this refers to a specific translation/edition or another chapter/text, it's not in this corpus version. The corpus covers Manusmṛti comprehensively (12 chapters), but no such detail exists in ch. 5.

[^1]: *Manusmṛti*, ch. 5, full chapter.[https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/]

[^2]: E.g., [p-0e826f] (Mn 5.41): "In a *madhuparka* and in a sacrifice, and in a rite for the ancestors and gods." [https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/#p-0e826f]; also Mn 5.27, 5.32, 5.39–41 (no wood/fuel mentioned). [https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/05/translation/#p-77c420]

[^3]: E.g., *Manusmṛti* ch. 4 [p-a6dada] on *Pindanvaharyaka* śrāddha. [https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/04/translation/#p-a6dada]

[^4]: *Yājñavalkya Smṛti* ch. 1 [p-c703b6]. [https://falsafa.corpus/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/01/translation/#p-c703b6]
